title: PropertyGroup Object
description: Represents a group of properties and provides access to the properties.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: Returns the specified property from the group using an index into the group.

methods.name: itemById
methods.description: Returns the specified property from the group using the unique ID of the property. The ID is consistent and can't be modified by the user and isn't affected by localization.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Returns the specified Property using the name of the property.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of properties within the group.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Returns the unique ID of this property.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Returns the name of this group as seen in the user interface. This name is localized and can change based on the current language

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: parent
properties.description: Returns the parent of this group. Typically this will be a Component.

related_samples.name: Library Item API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to examine library items using the API.
To use the sample, create a new Python or C++ script and copy and paste this code, replacing the existing default code. The script will search for and record all components and library items in the current project. They are displayed in a dialog when the script has finished.

classType.title: PropertyGroup.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: PropertyGroup.item Method
item.description: Returns the specified property from the group using an index into the group.
item.return_description: Returns the specified group or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the property within the group where the first item is 0.

related_samples.name: Library Item API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to examine library items using the API.
To use the sample, create a new Python or C++ script and copy and paste this code, replacing the existing default code. The script will search for and record all components and library items in the current project. They are displayed in a dialog when the script has finished.

itemById.title: PropertyGroup.itemById Method
itemById.description: Returns the specified property from the group using the unique ID of the property. The ID is consistent and can't be modified by the user and isn't affected by localization.
itemById.return_description: Returns the specified Property or null if the ID doesn't match a property within the collection.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique ID of the property.

itemByName.title: PropertyGroup.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Returns the specified Property using the name of the property.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified property or null if the name doesn't match a property within the group.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the property to return. This is the name as seen in the user interface and may be localized.

count.title: PropertyGroup.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of properties within the group.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = propertyGroup_var.count

id.title: PropertyGroup.id Property
id.description: Returns the unique ID of this property.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = propertyGroup_var.id

isValid.title: PropertyGroup.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = propertyGroup_var.isValid

name.title: PropertyGroup.name Property
name.description: Returns the name of this group as seen in the user interface. This name is localized and can change based on the current language

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = propertyGroup_var.name

# Set the value of the property.
propertyGroup_var.name = propertyValue

objectType.title: PropertyGroup.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = propertyGroup_var.objectType

parent.title: PropertyGroup.parent Property
parent.description: Returns the parent of this group. Typically this will be a Component.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = propertyGroup_var.parent
